results.description: Happy path -> working exactly on the planned feature
results.feedback: The developer is implementing the planned feature by fetching the ETH balance using ethers.js.

results.description: Mild drift -> working on related but non-planned work
results.feedback: The developer is working on styling and animations, which is unrelated to fetching the wallet balance.

results.description: Severe drift -> working on a completely different feature
results.feedback: The developer is working on user authentication, which is unrelated to fetching wallet balances.

results.description: Debugging the active feature -> not drift
results.feedback: The developer is addressing a technical issue (CORS) that is necessary to connect to the Ethereum RPC, which is part of the planned feature.

results.description: Researching the active task -> not drift
results.feedback: The developer is currently researching the necessary tools and methods to implement the planned feature.

results.description: Working on a future backlog feature -> severe drift
results.feedback: The developer is working on transaction history, which is not directly related to displaying the ETH balance.

results.description: Setting up tooling related to active task -> not drift
results.feedback: The developer is setting up the development environment, which is part of the planned feature.

results.description: Mild drift -> adding nice-to-have while task is active
results.feedback: The developer is working on dark mode, which is a nice-to-have and not part of the core planned feature.

results.description: Refactoring code from the active feature -> not drift
results.feedback: The developer is refactoring the RPC connection logic, which is related to fetching the wallet balance, so they have not drifted.

results.description: Working on backend while frontend task is active -> severe drift
results.feedback: The developer is building a backend, which is outside the scope of the planned feature.

results.description: User pastes a code snippet -> sufficient immediately
Vague but mentions library name -> sufficient
Describes frustration with no technical detail -> insufficient
Describes a concrete action but for wrong feature -> insufficient
